title: Inheritance Basics
subtitle: Build new classes on top of existing ones
learning_objectives: Create a child class that inherits from a parent class
Use super().__init__() to call the parent initialiser
Add new attributes and methods in the child class
Use isinstance() to check an object's class

sections.heading: What is Inheritance?
sections.content:
Inheritance lets you create a new class based on an existing one. The new class (child) automatically gets all the attributes and methods of the existing class (parent). You only need to add or change what is different.

Think of it like this: a Dog IS AN Animal. A Cat IS AN Animal. Both share common animal traits (eat, sleep, breathe) but have their own specific behaviours.
sections.code_output:
Rex is eating.
Rex says: Woof!

sections.heading: Calling super().__init__()
sections.content: When a child class has its own __init__, you usually call super().__init__() to run the parent's __init__ first. This sets up all the parent's attributes before you add the child's own.

sections.heading: isinstance() Check
sections.content: Use isinstance(obj, ClassName) to check if an object is an instance of a class. A child object is also an instance of the parent class.
sections.code_example:
class Animal:
    pass

class Dog(Animal):
    pass

rex = Dog()
print(isinstance(rex, Dog))    # True
print(isinstance(rex, Animal)) # True  — Dog IS AN Animal

exercises.title: Create a Child Class
exercises.prompt: Define a Vehicle class with __init__(make, speed) and a describe() method that prints 'Make: X, Speed: Y'. Then define a Car class that inherits from Vehicle and adds a doors attribute (default 4). Create car = Car('Toyota', 120) and call car.describe(), then print car.doors.
hints: class Car(Vehicle): — Vehicle in parentheses means inherit

exercises.title: Animal Hierarchy
exercises.prompt: Create an Animal class with __init__(name) and a describe() method that prints 'I am an animal named X'. Create a Cat class inheriting Animal, with a purr() method that prints 'X says: Purrr'. Create cat = Cat('Whiskers'), call describe() and purr().
hints: The starter code is correct — just run it!
Cat inherits __init__ from Animal so it works without its own
self.name inside purr() works because Cat inherited it from Animal

tests.value: I am an animal named Whiskers

quiz.question: How do you make a class Dog inherit from Animal?
quiz.explanation: In Python, you specify the parent class in parentheses after the child class name: class Dog(Animal):. This is the inheritance syntax.

quiz.question: What does super().__init__() do inside a child class's __init__?
options: Calls the parent class's __init__ to set up inherited attributes
quiz.explanation: super().__init__() calls the parent class's __init__ method, which sets up all the attributes defined there. Without this call, the parent's attributes would never be created.

completion_message: Great work! Inheritance is a powerful tool — you can now build class hierarchies!
